channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] not much happened today
item.description: [AINews] not much happened today

item.title: [Latent Space] AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency
item.description: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Autoresearch and the tension between AI and human agency

item.title: [Hacker News] Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot
item.description: Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BlockPilot: Instance-Adaptive Policy Learning for Diffusion-based Speculative Decoding
item.description: BlockPilot adapts block sizes per sample for speculative decoding, improving inference speed across inputs with negligible overhead.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Linguistic Bias Mitigation for Spoofing Detection via Gradient Reversal and A Variational Information Bottleneck
item.description: Gradient reversal and variational information bottleneck framework reduces spoofing detection errors by 36.2% by mitigating linguistic bias.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Clinically Structured Rank-Gated LoRA for Cross-Benchmark Medical Question Answering
item.description: BiRG-LoRA achieves 69.31% accuracy on medical QA benchmarks with input-conditioned rank-gating, using 28% fewer trainable parameters than MoELoRA.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Revising RVL-CDIP: Quantifying Errors and Test-Train Overlap
item.description: Analysis of RVL-CDIP reveals 12% label errors and 35% test-train overlap; correcting errors improves out-of-distribution generalization up to 14%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Team MKC at CLPsych 2026: Capturing and Characterizing Mental Health Changes through Social Media Timeline Dynamics
item.description: An LLM-based pipeline analyzes sequential social media posts to detect and characterize mental health changes through temporal modeling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Building an ASR Solution for Training and Assessing Children's Reading
item.description: Open-source ASR system for Bambara children's reading reduces error rate by roughly 50% with public benchmark and classroom validation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] FinPersona-Bench: A Benchmark for Longitudinal Psychometric Stability of Autonomous Financial Agents
item.description: FinPersona-Bench demonstrates that financial LLM agents progressively ignore behavioral mandates as market context accumulates over time.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] AutoTrainess: Teaching Language Models to Improve Language Models Autonomously
item.description: AutoTrainess enables autonomous post-training of language models through structured interfaces for planning, data prep, training, and evaluation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Robust Text Watermarking for Large Language Models via Dual Semantic Embeddings
item.description: Dual-Embedding Watermarking leverages token and context embeddings to create LLM watermarks that remain detectable through paraphrasing and translation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CLExEval: A Human-in-the-Loop Framework for Qualitative Evaluation of LLM Clinical Reasoning
item.description: CLExEval human-in-the-loop evaluation identifies three LLM failure patterns in clinical reasoning: verbosity bias, knowledge paradox, and reasoning-output mismatches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Tone-Conditioned Curriculum Learning for Low-Resource Bantu Speech Recognition
item.description: Tone-conditioned curriculum learning framework improves speech recognition for six Southern Bantu languages, with model performance varying across language groups.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Moral Safety in LLMs: Exposing Performative Compliance with Puzzled Cues
item.description: LLM fairness evaluations overestimate moral safety; models show performative compliance, appearing fair when demographic identity is explicit but less fair when inferred.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Overview of the TalentCLEF 2026: Skill and Job Title Intelligence for Human Capital Management
item.description: The TalentCLEF 2026 challenge evaluated 113 teams on NLP tasks for job-candidate matching and job-skill classification in human capital management.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Cross-lingual Relation Extraction with Large Language Models: Zero-Shot, Few-Shot, and Fine-Tuned Evaluation on Romanian
item.description: Cross-lingual relation extraction evaluation on Romanian shows QLoRA fine-tuning reduces English-Romanian gap, but smaller encoders match large LLM performance with fewer parameters.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Seeing Is Not Sharing: Some Vision-Language Models Overestimate Common Ground in Asymmetric Dialogue
item.description: Vision-language models overestimate common ground in dialogue, treating accessible content as mutually understood rather than tracking dialogue-based grounding.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Adapting Foundation ASR Models to Dysarthric Speech: A Case Study
item.description: Foundation ASR model fine-tuning on dysarthric speaker data reduced word error rate to 9.7%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] STEB: Style Text Embedding Benchmark
item.description: New STEB benchmark standardizes style embedding evaluation with 96 datasets across 7 languages, finding semantic embeddings fail at stylistic tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] CHERRY: Compressed Hierarchical Experts with Recurrent Representational Yield
item.description: Study combines selective token supervision (4.5x efficiency), layer compression (2.5x parameter reduction), and mixture-of-experts for efficient language model training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Explicit Fuzzy Logic in the Feed-Forward Layer: Self-Forgetting Quantifiers Discover Legible Grammatical-Licensing Detectors
item.description: Explicit fuzzy logic replaces standard FFN layers, improving interpretability while maintaining performance and detecting grammatical licensing patterns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Theory of Mind and Persuasion Beyond Conversation: Assessing the Capacity of LLMs to Induce Belief States via Planning and Action
item.description: Researchers evaluate LLMs' ability to induce belief states through non-conversational planning; GPT-5 succeeds on 80% of tasks, outperforming human participants.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LuxEmo: Expressive Text-to-Speech Corpus for Luxembourgish
item.description: Researchers release LuxEmo, a 21-hour expressive Luxembourgish speech corpus with four emotions, benchmarking five text-to-speech systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DigitalCoach: Communication and Grounding Gaps in Human and Agentic Computer Use Coaching
item.description: DigitalCoach dataset reveals that AI models coach differently than humans, providing direct instructions but lacking explanations and visual grounding when teaching software use.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
item.description: Research demonstrates scalable behavior cloning for browser agents by extracting reusable skills from user interaction logs and organizing them in skill graphs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
item.description: SkillComposer enables LLM agents to automatically select and order skills for complex tasks by jointly predicting skill subset, count, and execution sequence.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
item.description: LLMs make data referencing errors when processing tables; a lightweight critic model detects them with 78.2% F1 and improves answer accuracy up to 12%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
item.description: Reinforcement learning with metacognitive feedback calibrates LLM confidence scores to align expressed with actual uncertainty.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
item.description: Language models trained on fixed counterfactual explanations generate explanations faithful to their current behavior despite behavioral shifts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ASR-Agnostic Multimodal Spectrotemporal Modeling for Early Dementia Detection
item.description: Speech-based dementia detection framework operating on spectrograms uses spectrotemporal displacement fields and multimodal fusion, achieving up to 83.9% accuracy across languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Emergent Culture in Minimal LLM Systems
item.description: Three LLM agents spontaneously develop cooperation and complex cultural artifacts using only message exchange and a shared decaying text store.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ViTL: Temporal Logic-Guided Zero-Shot Natural Language Navigation via Vision-Language Models
item.description: ViTL framework enables zero-shot navigation of robots following multi-target natural language commands with temporal constraints using vision-language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Representation as a Bottleneck for Mechanistic Interpretability: The Manifestation Unit Protocol
item.description: Researchers introduce Manifestation Units, a structured protocol for organizing and querying neural network component analyses across vision and language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SNAP-FM: Sparse Nonlinear Accelerated Projection for Physics-Constrained Generative Modeling
item.description: Sparse GPU-based optimization accelerates physics-constrained generative models while enforcing conservation laws and physical constraints at inference.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SemiScope: Disentangling Classifier Tuning and Joint Optimization in Semi-Supervised Security Classification
item.description: Optimizing the downstream classifier alone performs as well as optimizing the full semi-supervised learning pipeline for security classification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Filtered Mixture-of-Generators for Fully Synthetic Survival Training
item.description: FoGS improves synthetic survival data by selecting samples from multiple generators based on ensemble survival model plausibility scoring.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GRPO, Dr. GRPO, and DAPO Are Three Operations on One Number: The Group-Standard-Deviation Identity
item.description: GRPO, Dr. GRPO, and DAPO are mathematically equivalent methods for adjusting language model training signal via answer disagreement's standard deviation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] EVOTS: Evolutionary Transformer Search for Time Series Forecasting
item.description: EVOTS proposes evolutionary neural architecture search to discover task-adaptive Transformer models for multivariate time-series forecasting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FRAME: Learning the Adaptation Domain with a Mixture of Fractional-Fourier Experts
item.description: Fractional-Fourier Mixture of Experts enables parameter-efficient fine-tuning by learning optimal transformation domains, interpolating between spatial and Fourier bases for better task adaptation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Verifiable Rewards for Calibrated Probabilistic Forecasting
item.description: RL model with verifiable state-conditioned rewards reaches betting-market calibration for NFL win probability without labeled training data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Scaling Up Thermodynamic AI Models
item.description: Scalable backpropagation method for training thermodynamic AI models on Ising hardware achieves 94.9% CIFAR-10 and 76.0% CIFAR-100 accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TallyTrain: Communication-Efficient Federated Distillation
item.description: TallyTrain reduces federated learning communication using hard-label consensus instead of soft-label distillation, achieving three orders of magnitude compression gains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Play Like Champions: Counterfactual Feedback Generation in Latent Space
item.description: StarCraft II framework generates counterfactual feedback paths to improve human players using VAE trained on professional replays.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TRIE: An Evaluation Framework for Stochastic PDE Surrogates
item.description: TRIE framework evaluates stochastic PDE surrogates, finding generative models best capture long-time statistics and uncertainty compared to deterministic or approximate methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] StateFlow: Dual-State Recurrent Modeling for Long-Horizon Time Series Forecasting
item.description: StateFlow extends VARNN with dual-state recurrent modeling to capture hidden-state and residual-memory trajectories for long-horizon time series forecasting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Device Passport: Enabling Spatio-Temporal Pretrained Models to Generalize Across Input Layouts
item.description: Device Passport improves biosignal foundation model generalization across different hardware layouts using channel embeddings that combine functional activity and metadata.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Distributionally Robust Linear Regression With Block Lewis Weights
item.description: Algorithm using block Lewis weights solves group distributionally robust least squares problems more efficiently than interior point methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learning dynamical systems from noisy data with Weak-form Kernel Ridge Regression
item.description: Weak-form Kernel Ridge Regression combines weak formulation with kernel learning to effectively predict dynamical systems from noisy data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Validating Causal Abstraction Metrics on Simulated Complex Systems
item.description: New metric validates causal abstraction validity by testing faithfulness over unmapped variables, outperforming 30 alternatives in complex system benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Entropy-Regularized Probabilistic Gates for Sparse Model Discovery in Scarce-Data Federated Learning
item.description: Entropy-regularized probabilistic gates improve sparse model learning in federated settings with limited data and high-dimensional features.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Testing Frontier Large Language Models' Physics Literacy in Parallel Physical Worlds
item.description: Frontier LLMs show poor physics reasoning in unfamiliar frameworks, correctly predicting change direction but frequently computing wrong quantitative values.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Understanding Guest Preferences and Optimizing Two-sided Marketplaces: Airbnb as an Example
item.description: Airbnb applies causal inference and economic modeling to understand guest price sensitivity for optimizing pricing and personalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] EPC: A Standardized Protocol for Measuring Evaluator Preference Dynamics in LLM Agent Systems
item.description: EPC standardizes measurement of evaluator preference coupling in LLM agents, enabling reproducible cross-evaluator and model comparisons.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Generative Modeling of Quantum Distribution with Functional Flow Matching
item.description: Researchers propose Quantum Flow Matching, a generative model that learns quantum distributions using spin Wigner functions and functional flow matching techniques.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mapping the Evaluation Frontier: An Empirical Survey of the Bias-Reliability Tradeoff Across Eleven Evaluator-Agent Conditions
item.description: Survey of 11 LLM evaluator conditions confirms tradeoff: evaluator coupling, strategy diversity, and measurement reliability cannot be simultaneously optimized.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Watermarking for Proprietary Dataset Protection
item.description: Watermarking techniques achieve comparable training data membership detection to loss-based methods in language models, particularly with high subset exposure.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] K-Inverse-RFM: A Modified RFM that Bridges the Gap to Neural Networks for Data-Corrupted Mathematical Tasks
item.description: K-Inverse-RFM uses label transformation to enable Recursive Feature Machines to match or exceed neural network performance on noisy and imbalanced mathematical tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PRISM: Prioritized Channel Importance with Semi-supervised Domain Adaptation for Cross-Subject EEG Emotion Recognition
item.description: PRISM framework uses channel weighting and semi-supervised domain adaptation to improve cross-subject EEG emotion recognition with limited labeled data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SAOT: Self-Supervised Continual Graph Learning with Structure-Aware Optimal Transport
item.description: SAOT framework uses optimal transport theory to preserve global relational structure in self-supervised continual graph learning, achieving 5-15% accuracy improvements over baseline methods.
